channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: The Desert Shield: Why the UAE is Betting on India's BrahMos and Akashteer

media:content.media:description: UAE agreed to unlock up to $20 billion in funds for Iran in exchange for a halt to the attacks

item.description: The UAE has traditionally bought its weapons from Western countries. So, why are they suddenly so interested in Indian defence technology?
item.content:encoded: The UAE has traditionally bought its weapons from Western countries. So, why are they suddenly so interested in Indian defence technology?

item.title: Explained: Why Petrol Prices in India Don't Follow Crude's Trajectory

media:content.media:description: Over the past two decades, global crude oil prices have seen sharp ups and downs

item.description: Crude oil prices that shot up amid the Middle East war has started to return to their pre-war levels.
item.content:encoded: Crude oil prices that shot up amid the Middle East war has started to return to their pre-war levels.

item.title: Ukraine Has An "Army Of Witches". They Are Killing Russians At Will

media:content.media:description: Ukrainian female soldiers wearing heels while taking part in the the military parade rehearsal in Kyiv.

item.description: Over four years, the war in Ukraine has changed, and with it, its people and tactics. It is now fought in the steady accumulation of small pieces of information that eventually become a set of coordinates and, shortly afterwards, a drone strike.
item.content:encoded: Over four years, the war in Ukraine has changed, and with it, its people and tactics. It is now fought in the steady accumulation of small pieces of information that eventually become a set of coordinates and, shortly afterwards, a drone strike.

item.title: Hormuz Ship Traffic Highest Since Start Of Middle East War: Report

media:content.media:description: LNG operators had previously been very cautious about transiting the strait.

item.description: At least 37 commodity carriers transited the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, a record level since the start of the Middle East war in late February, according to data from the maritime tracking firm Kpler.
item.content:encoded: At least 37 commodity carriers transited the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, a record level since the start of the Middle East war in late February, according to data from the maritime tracking firm Kpler.

item.title: Iran, Oman Plan Talks on Hormuz Control, Service Charges

media:content.media:description: The statement followed a high-level meeting between their top diplomats in Muscat

item.description: Oman and Iran will study the future administration of the Strait of Hormuz and the costs to be charged for services provided, they said in a joint statement Tuesday, insisting on sovereignty over the vital waterway.
item.content:encoded: Oman and Iran will study the future administration of the Strait of Hormuz and the costs to be charged for services provided, they said in a joint statement Tuesday, insisting on sovereignty over the vital waterway.

item.title: Eiffel Tower To Close Early Due To Severe Heatwave
item.description: The Eiffel Tower, one of the most visited tourist attractions in the world, was on Tuesday set to close early because of a severe heatwave, its operator said.
item.content:encoded: The Eiffel Tower, one of the most visited tourist attractions in the world, was on Tuesday set to close early because of a severe heatwave, its operator said.

item.title: Tarique Rahman Begins China Visit, Teesta Project Talks On The Agenda

media:content.media:description: India will be closely watching any announcement on the Teesta plan

item.description: On 24 June, Tarique Rahman will hold a meeting with the prime minister of Kazakhstan and later depart Dalian for Beijing by high-speed train later in the day. Rahman will also meet leaders of other countries on the trip.
item.content:encoded: On 24 June, Tarique Rahman will hold a meeting with the prime minister of Kazakhstan and later depart Dalian for Beijing by high-speed train later in the day. Rahman will also meet leaders of other countries on the trip.

item.title: "Absolutely Terrified" Tourists Run For Safety As Volcano Erupts During Hike In Guatemala
item.description: Volcan de Fuego, located about 43 km southwest of Guatemala City, is the country's most active volcano.
item.content:encoded: Volcan de Fuego, located about 43 km southwest of Guatemala City, is the country's most active volcano.

item.title: Every AI Prompt Costs Water. Nvidia's 'Liquid Coolant' Tech Could Change That

media:content.media:description: Nvidia says its latest AI servers could significantly reduce the need for water-based cooling

item.description: As AI systems become more powerful, the data centres that run them require enormous amounts of energy and cooling. The United Nations has warned that by the end of this decade, AI data centres could consume as much water as 1.3 billion people.
item.content:encoded: As AI systems become more powerful, the data centres that run them require enormous amounts of energy and cooling. The United Nations has warned that by the end of this decade, AI data centres could consume as much water as 1.3 billion people.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Why Pakistan Is Becoming Harder To Isolate - Despite India's Push
item.description: Pakistan's willingness to sell itself to the highest bidder explains its curious positioning in the world. India's only option now is to get creative as it tries to isolate it globally.
item.content:encoded: Pakistan's willingness to sell itself to the highest bidder explains its curious positioning in the world. India's only option now is to get creative as it tries to isolate it globally.

item.title: India, Israel Identify New Defence Projects To Build Joint Resilience

media:content.media:description: India and Israel finalise a strategic defence pact for joint development and resilience.

item.description: India and Israel advance defence ties with a new special strategic partnership framework.
item.content:encoded: India and Israel advance defence ties with a new special strategic partnership framework.

item.title: 20 Drown While Swimming In Unsupervised Areas In France Amid Heatwave

media:content.media:description: "There have been around 20 deaths since last weekend," French sports minister said

item.description: Much of France was set to experience temperatures around 40 degrees Celsius (104 degrees Fahrenheit) on Tuesday, forecaster MeteoFrance said.
item.content:encoded: Much of France was set to experience temperatures around 40 degrees Celsius (104 degrees Fahrenheit) on Tuesday, forecaster MeteoFrance said.

item.title: What Pakistan Could Gain From Brokering US-Iran Peace Talks

media:content.media:description: Pakistan is targeting economic growth of 4.0% and inflation of 8.2% for the coming fiscal year

item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir attended talks between Iran and the US in the Swiss town of Buergenstock last weekend
item.content:encoded: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir attended talks between Iran and the US in the Swiss town of Buergenstock last weekend

item.title: "Confusing Negotiators": JD Vance On Iran's Araghchi's On-Camera 'Snub'

media:content.media:description: Abbas Araghchi was seen greeting Shehbaz Sharif, while JD Vance remained behind

item.description: The visuals from the talks had sparked attention as JD Vance was present in the room while Abbas Araghchi appeared to ignore him and walk away without any interaction.
item.content:encoded: The visuals from the talks had sparked attention as JD Vance was present in the room while Abbas Araghchi appeared to ignore him and walk away without any interaction.

item.title: Japan Struggles With More Children Getting Involved In Upskirting Crimes

media:content.media:description: A growing number of children are being linked to upskirting offences in Japan

item.description: According to police data cited by CNN, reported voyeurism cases involving minors jumped nearly sixfold in 2024 compared to the previous year and increased again in 2025.
item.content:encoded: According to police data cited by CNN, reported voyeurism cases involving minors jumped nearly sixfold in 2024 compared to the previous year and increased again in 2025.

item.title: The Mistakes That Sealed Keir Starmer's Fate

media:content.media:description: Keir Starmer announces resignation due to low popularity and electoral setbacks.

item.description: Keir Starmer resigns as UK Prime Minister after poor election results and political difficulties.
item.content:encoded: Keir Starmer resigns as UK Prime Minister after poor election results and political difficulties.

item.title: US Teacher Who Sexually Abused Students Later Blackmailed Over OnlyFans Account For Better Grades

media:content.media:description: Maris Nichols faces multiple charges after allegedly having sex with students.

item.description: Maris Nichols, a 25-year-old teacher at Alexander High School, has been accused of sexually abusing students.
item.content:encoded: Maris Nichols, a 25-year-old teacher at Alexander High School, has been accused of sexually abusing students.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | The Great Surrender: Why This Is America's Worst Humiliation In Over 50 Years
item.description: There is no precedent in recent US history for an American president accepting such humiliating terms as in the Iran deal.
item.content:encoded: There is no precedent in recent US history for an American president accepting such humiliating terms as in the Iran deal.

item.title: With Iran, US Signing Peace Deal, Where Does That Leave Netanyahu?

media:content.media:description: Netanyahu confronts dilemma balancing US alliance and military actions against Hezbollah

item.description: Netanyahu confronts dilemma balancing US alliance and military actions against Hezbollah
item.content:encoded: Netanyahu confronts dilemma balancing US alliance and military actions against Hezbollah

item.title: India, US Hold Ministerial-Level Trade Pact Talks

media:content.media:description: Bilateral trade discussions begin between India and US officials.

item.description: India and US start talks on first phase of bilateral trade agreement in New Delhi.
item.content:encoded: India and US start talks on first phase of bilateral trade agreement in New Delhi.
